error.signature: MongoServerError: Index build failed: E11000 duplicate key error collection: mydb.users index: email_1 dup key: { email: null }

verdict.summary: Index creation failed. Commonly due to duplicate values (including null) when creating a unique index, or insufficient disk/memory.

dead_ends.action: Force the index build with a hidden flag
dead_ends.why_fails: There is no force flag. The duplicate data must be resolved before a unique index can be created.

dead_ends.action: Drop and recreate the collection to fix duplicates
dead_ends.why_fails: Destroying data is not necessary. Clean up duplicates with an aggregation pipeline and targeted deletes.

workarounds.action: Find and remove duplicate values before creating the unique index
Use a partial index to exclude null values from uniqueness
workarounds.how: db.users.createIndex({ email: 1 }, { unique: true, partialFilterExpression: { email: { $exists: true, $ne: null } } })

workarounds.action: Build the index in the background to avoid blocking operations
workarounds.how: db.users.createIndex({ email: 1 }, { unique: true })  # MongoDB 4.2+ builds in background by default
